description: Migration adds primary-email label metadata to `contacts` while preserving the existing required `contacts.email` column.
Migration creates reusable email-label definition storage and the additional-email table.
The database rejects two contacts in the same tenant using the same primary email.
The database rejects an additional email row that duplicates another contact's primary email in the same tenant.
The database rejects an additional email row that duplicates another contact's additional email in the same tenant.
The same normalized email address may still exist in different tenants.
Shared contact interfaces and `@alga-psa/types` expose scalar primary `email`, primary label metadata, and `additional_email_addresses` together.
Validation accepts a contact with a labeled primary email and no additional emails.
Validation rejects an additional email row that repeats the primary email.
Validation rejects duplicate additional email rows within the same payload.
Validation rejects malformed additional email rows or label combinations that violate the canonical/custom label rules.
Creating a contact persists primary-email label metadata and additional-email rows and hydrates them correctly on readback.
Updating a contact to promote an additional email swaps the old primary email into the child table and moves the promoted email into `contacts.email` with its label metadata.
Deleting the primary/default email directly is rejected until another additional email is promoted.
Tenant-scoped custom email labels are created or reused case-insensitively for both primary and additional emails.
Orphaned custom email labels can be cleaned up once no primary or additional email rows reference them.
Contact created and updated domain-event payloads include primary-email label metadata and additional-email rows while keeping summary email fields on `email`.
ContactEmailAddressesEditor renders a pinned primary row plus additional rows and normalizes their order on save.
ContactEmailAddressesEditor supports canonical label selection and custom label suggestions for both primary and additional rows.
Contact detail edit flow saves the hybrid email payload instead of a single unlabeled scalar email input.
Contact detail view renders the primary/default email distinctly and lists additional emails with labels.
QuickAddContact creates a contact with labeled primary email data and optional additional-email rows.
QuickAddClient inline contact creation saves the hybrid email payload for the new contact.
Contacts and client-contact lists continue rendering `contacts.email` as the visible summary email.
ContactPicker and related summary pickers continue showing the primary email while remaining compatible with the richer contact contract.
ContactService and query actions match contacts when the search term hits an additional email row.
ContactService export still emits the primary `email` summary while additional-email aware queries remain available for filtering/search.
CSV template and preview copy describe the primary email plus additional-email fields correctly.
CSV import creates or updates a contact with a primary email and additional-email rows.
CSV export includes primary-email label metadata and the chosen additional-email representation.
`findContactByEmailAddress` returns a contact when only an additional email matches the lookup address.
`createOrFindContactByEmail` creates a new contact with the email stored in `contacts.email` and no child rows when no match exists.
Shared `EmailService.findContactByEmail` preserves both the matched sender email and the contact's primary/default `contacts.email` when the match came from an additional row.
Shared `EmailService.createOrFindContact` remains compatible by creating contacts with only a primary email in `contacts.email`.
Shared workflow `findContactByEmail` resolves a contact when the sender matches an additional email row.
Inbound email processing preserves contact authorship with the exact matched sender email when the contact matched through an additional email row.
Inbound email processing still uses `contacts.email` as the default outbound contact email even when the inbound match used an additional email.
Runtime email-action schemas and workflow mappings expose matched sender email separately from the primary/default contact email.
Workflow business-operation `contacts.find` and `contacts.search` match contacts through additional email rows while keeping summary email fields on `contact.email`.
REST contact create/update accepts scalar primary email plus label metadata and additional-email rows.
REST contact responses return scalar primary email, primary label metadata, and additional-email rows together.
n8n contact parsers, docs, and example payloads accept the hybrid email fields.
Integration and client-lookup helpers resolve contacts when only an additional email matches.
External sync adapters preserve `contacts.email` as the default billing/summary address while understanding additional emails where lookup logic requires them.
Factories, seeds, and test helpers can create contacts with primary-email labels and additional-email rows.
Portal invitation, registration, and client-user linking flows remain compatible because they continue to use `contacts.email` after additional emails are added or the default is swapped.
Ticket, project, survey, billing, and scheduling contact-email sends continue to use the current `contacts.email` after a default swap.
Watcher and other email-snapshot recipient behavior remains compatible under the hybrid model unless the surface explicitly re-resolves the contact from email input.
A DB-backed end-to-end regression covers create -> add additional email -> promote additional email to default -> lookup by both addresses -> verify uniqueness guards.
